# Code of Conduct

## Our Community

SkillHiive exists to build healthier online communities.

The way we collaborate on this project should reflect the same values we hope to encourage in the software itself.

We expect everyone participating in this community to contribute with empathy, curiosity, and respect.

---

## Our Values

### Respect

Treat everyone with dignity, regardless of experience, background, or opinion.

---

### Collaboration

We value cooperation over competition.

Ideas should be discussed openly and constructively.

---

### Kindness

Assume good intentions.

Offer feedback that helps people improve instead of discouraging them.

---

### Curiosity

Questions are encouraged.

Nobody is expected to know everything.

Learning is part of contributing.

---

## Expected Behaviour

We encourage:

- Being respectful during discussions.
- Giving constructive feedback.
- Welcoming newcomers.
- Helping others learn.
- Keeping conversations focused and productive.
- Accepting mistakes with humility.

---

## Unacceptable Behaviour

This includes, but is not limited to:

- Harassment or discrimination.
- Personal attacks.
- Trolling.
- Hate speech.
- Threats or intimidation.
- Deliberately disruptive behaviour.
- Publishing private information without permission.
- Spam or promotional content unrelated to the project.

---

## Maintainer Responsibilities

Maintainers are responsible for fostering a welcoming and productive environment.

They may:

- Remove inappropriate comments.
- Close discussions that become unproductive.
- Request changes to contributions.
- Temporarily or permanently restrict participation when necessary.

These decisions are made to protect the health of the community—not to silence differing opinions.

---

## Enforcement

If you experience or witness behaviour that violates this Code of Conduct, please contact the maintainers privately.

All reports will be handled respectfully and, where possible, confidentially.

---

## Our Philosophy

SkillHiive was created because we believe online spaces can be healthier.

That begins here.

Every issue, pull request, discussion, and conversation contributes to the culture of this project.

Let's build software—and a community—that people genuinely enjoy being part of.
